question_prompt: Q10: How often have you needed to “ghost” a job applicant for these reasons?
panels: Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). I need to prioritize other candidates who are more likely to help me achieve my recruitment performance goals
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). The candidate is not a good fit for the role or the company culture
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). The candidate's salary expectations are beyond the budget
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). The high volume of candidates makes it difficult to respond to everyone
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). We already hired someone else for the position
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). We are no longer hiring for the role
Q10: (By “ghost” we mean breaking contact with a candidate without an explanation). We are still deciding on the right candidate
